question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoRenewPeriod field returned by account() to determine the exact time at which auto-renewal fees will be charged, then time transactions to occur just before renewal to avoid fee deduction from their balance?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user read the expirationTimestamp field from account() to determine when an account will expire and auto-renewal fees will be assessed, using this information to time fee-sensitive operations outside the design parameters for fee collection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user read the stakedAccount field from account() to map staking relationships between accounts, identifying accounts whose staking configuration affects fee calculations, and use this to predict or manipulate fee-relevant reward distributions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user query the autoRenewAccount field to identify which account pays auto-renewal fees on behalf of another, then target the auto-renew account with balance-draining transactions to prevent fee payment and cause unintended account expiration?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: balance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that balance() is a @SchemaMapping resolver that receives the Account object already populated by account(), and by requesting balance with an extreme unit like GIGABAR, cause the returned value to be 0 even when the account has a non-trivial balance, misleading any downstream fee-enforcement that reads this field?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxAutomaticTokenAssociations field exposed by account() to infer the maximum number of implicit token associations (each of which incurs a fee), and use this to predict the maximum fee burden on an account, enabling targeted fee exhaustion attacks?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user read the nonce (ethereumNonce) field from account() to track Ethereum-compatible transaction counts, using this to predict when fee-relevant nonce-based protections reset and submit transactions at fee-advantageous nonce values?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/controller/AccountController.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getByIdAndType() calls findById() (which does NOT filter deleted accounts) while getByAliasAndType() and getByEvmAddressAndType() use native queries that DO filter deleted accounts, creating an inconsistency where a deleted account's fee-relevant balance is accessible via entityId lookup but not via alias/evmAddress?
